However, this is standard for such factory classes and not indicative of malicious intent in the code itself.\n   - No assumptions about vulnerabilities are made; the code does not perform any untrusted data handling or dynamic code execution.\n\n6. **Overall Assessment:**\n   - The code is a standard, autogenerated ethers.js factory for a smart contract.\n   - No signs of malware, obfuscation, or malicious behavior.\n   - The risk scores are reasonable and align with the code's nature.\n   - The malware score should remain at 0, as no malicious activity is detected.\n\n**Final Recommendations:**\n- The malware score should stay at **0**.\n- The obfuscated score remains **0**.\n- The overall security risk remains very low, around **0.1 to 0.2**, primarily due to the inherent risk in connecting to arbitrary addresses, not because of the code itself.\n\n**My own estimate:**\n- **Malware score:** 0\n- **Obfuscated score:** 0\n- **Risk score:** ~0.1 (standard for contract interaction libraries, minimal risk)\n\n**Summary:**\nThe code is a typical, safe, autogenerated ethers.js factory for a smart contract.
